McGregor has likewise voiced his frustration with the organisation in recent times, reports All Out Fighting.

FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Alex Pereira provides update on Jon Jones fight after signing new UFC deal READ MORE :

Conor McGregor handed comeback blow as Nate Diaz added to Ronda Rousey undercard READ MORE :

O'Malley has been swift to defend the UFC leadership, and reckons the three aforementioned legends ought to be somewhat more appreciative. "It's crazy, you see all these top guys having issues with the UFC, and Ronda obviously is not in the UFC anymore, but talking s*** about it," O'Malley said on YouTube.

"Conor, the biggest star of all time, having issues with the UFC. Jon Jones, the GOAT, having issues with the UFC. It's crazy, all of these careers, who would Conor, Jones, and Ronda be without the UFC? Could they have been successful in other ventures? Yes. Could they have made as much money? Probably not. So it's weird. I get where they're coming from, too, if they think they're being treated unfair, speak up. One way I see it, but then it's like also you've got to be grateful for what you got from the UFC, from the business."

UFC star Harry Hardwick left disfigured after brutal fight as ambulance video shared READ MORE :

Francis Ngannou issues surprise response to Jon Jones demand READ MORE :

Rousey has refused to retract her scathing comments, with the former UFC champion recently targeting the promotion's chief business officer Hunter Campbell. Leading up to the forthcoming Netflix MMA event, the organisation has also confirmed the participation of fighters including Nate Diaz, Mike Perry and Francis Ngannou.

Article continues below

After the announcement of Diaz's first MMA bout in almost four years, Rousey couldn't resist another swipe at the UFC. In a message shared on social media, she posted: "Welcome to the revolution Nate Diaz, Mike Perry. It's the FIGHTERS vs the establishment - and we're gunna win! May 16 only on Netflix. PS Hunter Campbell p****d off the wrong b****. I'm coming for your job motherf***er."

Sports.title: Alex Pereira provides update on Jon Jones fight after signing new UFC deal
Sports.content:
Alex Pereira provides update on Jon Jones fight after signing new UFC deal

Alex Pereira is set to face Ciryl Gane for the interim heavyweight title in June, but the Brazilian is still holding out hope of a future showdown with Jon Jones

View Image Alex Pereira and Jon Jones have been tipped to fight each other

Alex Pereira remains convinced he will exchange blows with Jon Jones after the former dual-weight champion signed a new eight-fight contract with the UFC.

‌



'Poatan' confirmed he penned a fresh deal with the promotion ahead of his return to the octagon this June. Pereira will make history on June 14 when he faces Ciryl Gane for the interim heavyweight title at UFC Freedom 250 at the White House.

‌



The 38-year-old relinquished the 205lb belt in favour of moving up to heavyweight in pursuit of a super-fight with Jones. However, a clash between the pair failed to come to fruition after Dana White and the UFC couldn't reach terms with Jones. 'Bones' initially hung up his gloves, but executed a dramatic reversal after learning of the organisation's scheduled trip to Washington.

‌



The Brazilian's contest with Gane launches his new contract with the UFC, offering him the ideal chance to capture gold in another division. "Honestly, it was a pretty easy choice," Pereira said on The Ariel Helwani Show, reports All Out Fighting.

FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Conor McGregor handed comeback blow as Nate Diaz added to Ronda Rousey undercard READ MORE :

"When I expressed that I wanted to go [up in weight], I of course had to let go of one of the belts because they gave me a new contract. I'm really, really happy with my new contract. I want to thank [UFC matchmaker] Mick [Maynard], [UFC CBO] Hunter [Campbell] and [UFC CEO] Dana [White] for it. With this new deal that they gave to me, I don't care about letting go of the belt or anything like that. They took really good care of me."

After making the jump up another weight division, Pereira plans on remaining there for the long haul, insisting there's nothing more for him to achieve at light-heavyweight. "I could make light heavyweight again, but I don't think there's anything new for me there," Pereira explained. "Many guys just go in the heat of the moment, and they jump a weight class, they lose, they come back. No. I've been expressing for a long time that I've been wanting to fight at heavyweight. So, I think I'll stay around here."

UFC star Harry Hardwick left disfigured after brutal fight as ambulance video shared READ MORE :

Oleksandr Usyk opens door to stunning Jon Jones crossover MMA fight READ MORE :

Whilst the combat sports veteran pushed for a showdown with Jones, 'Poatan' revealed there were never any discussions with the UFC about facing Jones, though he remains optimistic a bout will ultimately materialise. "I believe [it's still possible]. As long as I win this fight, right?" he said.

Article continues below

"Because otherwise, it doesn't make too much sense. I'm a dangerous guy. Why would he put himself on the line? But I believe I will win in this fight. It's a big chance. I believe it happens because he has [asked] to be released from the UFC. I don't think the UFC's going to release him anytime soon. So, if he's going to fight, who's it going to be against? It's going to be against me."

Sports.title: Ronda Rousey's war of words with UFC continues after brutal six-word jibe
Sports.content:
Ronda Rousey's war of words with UFC continues after brutal six-word jibe

Ronda Rousey has launched another attack on the UFC ahead of her MMA return in May when she faces Gina Carano on Netflix

View Image Ronda Rousey speaks out (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Former UFC champion Ronda Rousey has launched another attack on her previous employers.

‌



Rousey is scheduled to compete in her first MMA bout in almost a decade this May when she faces fellow veteran Gina Carano in Los Angeles. The American hasn't fought since sustaining a devastating first-round knockout loss to Amanda Nunes at UFC 207 back in 2016.

‌



The 39-year-old trailblazed women's participation in the sport and became the UFC's inaugural female champion. Rousey has been in training over recent months and had been speculated to stage a dramatic comeback to the UFC octagon, but opted to align with Jake Paul's Most Valuable Promotions (MVP) for her MMA return.

‌



Following the announcement of her fighting comeback, the American criticised the UFC for underpaying their athletes and also condemned the promotion for failing to deliver the best possible matchups. In preparation for the forthcoming Netflix MMA showcase, the promotion has also recruited the likes of Nate Diaz, Mike Perry and Francis Ngannou to the card. After the confirmation of Diaz's first MMA contest in almost four years, Rousey couldn't help but take another swipe at the UFC, reports All Out Fighting.

FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

UFC star Harry Hardwick left disfigured after brutal fight as ambulance video shared READ MORE :

Francis Ngannou issues surprise response to Jon Jones demand READ MORE :

In a message shared on social media, she posted: "Welcome to the revolution Nate Diaz, Mike Perry. It's the FIGHTERS vs the establishment - and we're gunna win! May 16 only on Netflix. PS Hunter Campbell p****d off the wrong b****. I'm coming for your job motherf***er."

‌



UFC CBO Campbell's daily responsibilities include overseeing the majority of fight negotiations within the promotion, and appears to have been in discussions with 'Rowdy' regarding a comeback before she opted to sign a lucrative contract with MVP.

Speaking about her return to MMA, Rousey stated: "I guess it depends on how this one goes. I really want some more babies, and it's getting to the point in my life where I can't really be taking detours anymore.

Ilia Topuria unloads on Islam Makhachev after UFC White House fight snub READ MORE :

Tom Aspinall issues fight update after double eye surgery READ MORE :

"I really want to be fully present for my husband and my kids. It gets to the point where it's like, when are you going to be impressed enough with yourself to spend some time with your family. I feel like this is me trying to rewrite my ending with Gina and maybe it's a Lord of the Rings ending that keeps going on and on and on. Or maybe it's a grand finale... But I promised my husband this was definitely the last one."

Article continues below

She continued: "Once I found that love again, and I saw her [Carano] not doing well, I was like, you know what we both need to reclaim our bodily identity together and rewrite our own ending together.

"That night, I reached out to her, and it's been such a journey to get here, and so many obstacles. So many people tried to get between us and insert their own agenda. We went from barely knowing each other and respecting each other to being like, 'You know what, we are going to fight to fight each other'. I told her I will train you to fight me if I have to. Luckily, I didn't have to do that."

Sports.title: Gerwyn Price hints at break from darts after suffering final heartbreak
Sports.content:
Gerwyn Price hints at break from darts after suffering final heartbreak

Gerwyn Price lost 8-3 to Dutch star Wessel Nijman in the final of the European Darts Trophy, and the Iceman has suggested that he'll be taking a break from the sport

View 3 Images Gerwyn Price was beaten by Wessel Nijman (Image: Charles McQuillan/Getty Images)

Exhausted Gerwyn Price has admitted that he 'needs a break' from darts following his heavy defeat in the European Darts Trophy final. Price, 41, suffered a 8-3 loss on Sunday against Wessel Nijman, who'd enjoyed a stunning run to the final.

‌



Dutch star Nijman beat the likes of Josh Rock and Gian van Veen in Germany on his way to claiming the £35,000 jackpot in emphatic fashion. Meanwhile, Price was left to reflect on what might've been.

‌



The Iceman took to Instagram to confess that he's struggling with the increased demands on top-level darts players. "Great weekend for me, another final but very frustrating in the end," Price wrote on his story.

‌



"Onto Dublin this week. Need a break from this, I think. It's so tough mentally. But I'll push on, thanks for the support."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

Man Utd fan Luke Littler brutally trolls supporters of Premier League rivals in walk-on READ MORE :

For now, Price will be back in action on Thursday for night seven of Premier League Darts. After Sunday's final, the Welshman heaped praise on Nijman by saying: "Every time I play Wessel he plays brilliantly against me.

‌



"I lost to him in a ProTour final as well so fair play. It's been a long day. Playing the fourth game is tough - you are not sure whether to go back and chill or stay. I have been here since 11am so I didn't get to go back and chill out.

"I ran out of gas in the end. I didn't really play my best in the final but take nothing away from him - he was fantastic. Fair play, he has been playing brilliantly today and all year but I will get my own back."

Are there too many darts tournaments? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



3 View 3 Images Gerwyn Price took on Instagram (Image: Instagram/gerwynpriceiceman180) 3 View 3 Images His next event is in Dublin on Thursday (Image: Alex Livesey/Getty Images)

Just under a year ago, former world champion Luke Humphries admitted that he needed a break after darts began to feel like a 'chore'. Following his victory over Dirk van Duivenbode in the second round of the International Darts Open, Humphries admitted: "When I'm up on stage I am just feeling emotionless.

‌



"It's weird and it's me being honest to all the fans. I feel like my emotions are just all over the place. It's not that I don't want to be here.

It just seems a chore for me at the moment, it's just too much darts. I'm playing too much, I think I need a break. I'm emotionless and it is not good for my mental state.

"The emotion was drained from there. Usually I have the fight in me. Dirk, I felt like, was nervous and he gave the game away for me to get the win."

Article continues below

Sports.title: Newsboy’s horseracing tips for Wednesday’s three meetings, including Hereford Nap
Sports.content:
Newsboy’s horseracing tips for Wednesday’s three meetings, including Hereford Nap

Newsboy fancies his nap, Vol Royale, to win at Hereford and also hopes xxxxxxx can score at Huntingdon and complete his Daily Double

View 2 Images Hereford racecourse features a seven-race card on Wednesday where Newsboy's Nap, Vol Royale, runs in the 4.30

VOL ROYALE (4.30, Nap) is fancied to resume winning ways dropped in class and returning to his favourite track at Hereford on Wednesday. The six-year-old, trained by Kaley Woollacott, was never going at Chepstow last month, eventually trailing home seventh of nine under Brendan Powell.

‌



‌

Sports.title: Trent Alexander-Arnold gets update as Real Madrid ‘put six stars on chopping block’
Sports.content:
Trent Alexander-Arnold gets update as Real Madrid ‘put six stars on chopping block’

Former Liverpool star Trent Alexander-Arnold has struggled for form at Real Madrid but could be handed a major boost in the summer

View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold could be handed a Real Madrid boost in the summer transfer window. (Image: Florencia Tan Jun, Getty Images)

Trent Alexander-Arnold could receive a significant boost to his Real Madrid career this summer, with the club reportedly preparing to offload one of his main rivals for a starting berth. The full-back completed his switch from Liverpool to Real Madrid last summer, as his contract at Anfield expired.

‌



The Reds did secure a fee of approximately £10 million, after allowing the player to complete his move in time for last summer's Club World Cup. Alexander-Arnold featured for Real Madrid in the tournament after completing the transfer.

‌



The 27-year-old's introduction to life at Real Madrid has not been as seamless as he might have expected. Two separate injuries have curtailed his playing time, and he has been rotated in and out of the starting XI.

‌



Diogo Jota's widow in touching Liverpool tribute ahead of difficult new milestone READ MORE :

He has also faced some criticism for his displays when starting matches. His defensive capabilities are the aspect that has attracted the most scrutiny.

However, he could receive a lift this summer, with BILD reporting that Real are poised to complete a major overhaul of their squad during the summer transfer window. The shake-up would see as many as six players depart.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



One of those is reported to be legendary right-back Dani Carvajal. The 34-year-old is Alexander-Arnold's main competition in the squad, with the Spaniard selected ahead of him for the weekend's La Liga fixture against Elche.

A potential exit of Carvajal could provide Alexander-Arnold with the opportunity to establish himself as the primary right-back at the Bernabeu. A series of regular starts could potentially help him to find the form from his time at Liverpool, which earned him the transfer in the first place.

Other players reportedly on Real Madrid's sale list include defenders David Alaba and Antonio Rudiger, left-backs Ferland Mendy and Fran Garcia, as well as midfielder Eduardo Camavinga.

‌



View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold has received some criticism of his performances for Real Madrid this season. (Image: Soccrates Images, Getty Images)

Real Madrid and Alexander-Arnold return to action on Tuesday evening as they face Man City in the second leg of their Champions League round-of-16 encounter at the Etihad Stadium

The Spanish titans carry a 3-0 lead from last week's first leg courtesy of Federico Valverde's first-half hat-trick.

Article continues below

Alexander-Arnold was in the starting line-up for the first leg before being substituted for Carvajal with six minutes of normal time remaining.

Fans can watch the game on the TNT Sports Prime Video channel.

Sports.title: Chelsea vs PSG referee was questioned by police after drug and prostitution ring operation
Sports.content:
Chelsea vs PSG referee was questioned by police after drug and prostitution ring operation

A Champions League referee previously found himself unwittingly involved in a police operation.

View 2 Images Slavko Vincic was questioned by the police (Image: Soccrates Images, Getty Images)

Champions League referee Slavko Vincic previously found himself detained as part of a police operation targeting a prostitution and drug ring, though he soon proved his innocence. In May 2020, the Slovenian official, who will oversee the round of 16 second leg between Chelsea and PSG on Tuesday, was at a business lunch in Bosnia and Herzegovina when law enforcement intervened.

‌



Vincic explained that he was attending a business meeting and had accepted a lunch invitation. However, the chosen location proved to be unfortunate as police targeted unrelated parties as part of a prostitution and drugs crackdown.

‌



The referee was subsequently questioned as a witness and released by the police, being cleared of any wrongdoing. Speaking to Slovenian media, Vincic clarified he had no connection to any criminal activity.

‌



"I found myself on this ranch by chance. I have my own company, I was in Bosnia and Herzegovina for a business meeting," Vincic said at the time. "I accepted an invitation to lunch, which turned out to be my biggest mistake. I regret it.

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

"I was sitting at a table with my company, all of a sudden the police came and what happened, happened. I have nothing to do with the group that was arrested and detained, nor do my business partners.

‌



Chelsea vs PSG Champions League preview, team news and prediction READ MORE :

Thierry Henry embarrasses Liam Rosenior over his response to Alejandro Garnacho incident READ MORE :

"Yes, they really took us to the police, asked as witnesses, when it turned out that we didn't even know them, we were able to go."

A statement from Bijeljina police said: "During the search of the house and vehicles used by the suspects, 14 packages of cocaine, 10 pistols, three suits of body armour, over 10,000 euros in various currencies, phones and laptops were found and seized."

Following Vincic's release, Vlado Sajn, president of the Slovenian Football Referees Association, said: "According to the information we have gathered from official and unofficial sources and from Slavko himself, he is not suspected of anything. No charges have been filed against him."

‌



View 2 Images Vincic refereed Chelsea's win over Barcelona in November (Image: Marc Atkins/Getty Images)

The 46-year-old is an elite referee in European football, having officiated the 2024 Champions League final between Real Madrid and Borussia Dortmund at Wembley. He took charge of Chelsea's triumph over Barcelona in November, which saw Barca defender Roland Araujo dismissed for two yellow cards in the first half.

Vincic also oversaw PSG's draw with Newcastle United in January. Meanwhile, Liam Rosenior's side will be focused on overturning a three-goal deficit against the French giants at Stamford Bridge.

Sports.title: FIFA drop Iran statement and 'reject Mexico World Cup request' after Donald Trump comments
Sports.content:
FIFA drop Iran statement and 'reject Mexico World Cup request' after Donald Trump comments

With the escalating tensions between the USA and Iran dominating the build-up of this summer's World Cup, FIFA have reportedly rejected a request from Mexico

View 2 Images President Donald Trump recently sent a concerning message to the Iranian football team on social media (Image: YouTube/The Independent)

FIFA have rejected Mexico's offer to host Iran's World Cup matches not long after US President Donald Trump said the Middle Eastern country should not compete. The escalating conflict between the USA and Israel, and Iran is showing no sign of easing.

‌



Not long after Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in an airstrike, the country's sports minister insinuated they would not compete in this summer's competition across the USA, Mexico and Canada. However, no official decision has been made on the Middle Eastern nation's presence at the World Cup, with FIFA insisting they are still expected to play two of their group matches in Los Angeles and one in Seattle.

‌



But with the tension between Iran and the USA escalating, Mexico reportedly offered to play host to the Iranians for their matches. This request is understood to have been denied by FIFA.

‌



Major stadium refuses to host World Cup matches despite splashing out £100m READ MORE :

Iran ask for drastic Mexico World Cup switch after USA decision amid Middle East war READ MORE :

The Iranian Football Association recently claimed they were in talks to move their matches to the country. This offer proceeded Trump's recent Truth Social post, where he wrote: "The Iran National Soccer Team is welcome to The World Cup, but I really don’t believe it is appropriate that they be there, for their own life and safety. Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DONALD J TRUMP.”

It's ambiguous as to whether Trump was referring to the safety of Iranian players in Iran or in the USA. Mehdi Taj, the president of the Iranian FA, believes the US president had said he could not guarantee the players' safety.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



He took to X and wrote: "When Trump has explicitly stated that he cannot ensure the security of the Iranian national team, we will certainly not travel to America. We are negotiating with Fifa to hold Iran’s World Cup matches in Mexico.”

With their request to play in Mexico being denied, FIFA issued a further comment, insisting Iran will be welcome to compete in the USA. They said: "FIFA is in regular contact with all participating member associations, including IR Iran, to discuss planning for the FIFA World Cup 2026. FIFA is looking forward to all participating teams competing as per the match schedule announced.”

View 2 Images It's unclear as to whether Iran will compete in this summer's competition (Image: 2024 VCG)

‌



Iran face New Zealand on June 15 and Belgium on June 21, with both matches taking place in LA. Their final match is scheduled to be on June 26 in Seattle against Egypt.

FIFA do have the power to decide which country would take Iran's place, should they decide to pull out of the competition. Iraq and the United Arab Emirates are the next highest ranked countries from the Asian Football Confederation who are yet to qualify for the competition.

Windsor ⁠John, the head of the Asian Football Confederation, recently insisted Iran are still expected to make the journey to the USA for the competition. When questioned on the subject in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, he said: "It’s a very emotional moment.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

"Everybody’s saying a lot of things. At the end of the day, it’s the federation who should decide if they’re playing, and as of today, the federation has told us that they are going to the ⁠World Cup.

"They are our member, we want them to play. You know, they qualified … so we hope that they will solve their issues, whatever it ⁠is, and be able to participate.”
